title: System tests: work around common gating flake
body:
Fedora gating tests frequently flake one test, because
/proc/PID/comm is "exe" instead of "podman".

Deal with it. Don't block gating, don't force a waive.
And add some debugging output.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
We were not able to find or create Copr project `packit/containers-podman-24069` specified in the config with the following error:
```
Packit received HTTP 500 Internal Server Error from Copr Service. Check the Copr status page: https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/status/stats/, or ask for help in Fedora Build System matrix channel: https://matrix.to/#/#buildsys:fedoraproject.org.
```
---
Unless the HTTP status code above is >= 500,  please check your configuration for:

1. typos in owner and project name (groups need to be prefixed with `@`)
2. whether the project name doesn't contain not allowed characters (only letters, digits, underscores, dashes and dots must be used)
3. whether the project itself exists (Packit creates projects only in its own namespace)
4. whether Packit is allowed to build in your Copr project
5. whether your Copr project/group is not private
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
The 500 copr issue has been fixed but not deployed yet. The deployment is expected next week.
This smells like a result of the reexec code, which is going to exec a new copy of `/proc/self/exe` from within Podman. I don't think there's anything to do other than accommodate it.

Oh, interesting. That sure seems consistent with the failure, although I still can't fathom why it only happens in rootless fedora gating. If you have reasonable confidence that this is the issue, I will remove the debug code and add a better comment.
Hmmm. The rootless code will reexec itself in this way on first run of Podman to create the rootless pause process, so rootless makes sense. Fedora gating only? No clue about that.

> This smells like a result of the reexec code, which is going to exec a new copy of /proc/self/exe from within Podman. I don't think there's anything to do other than accommodate it.

I commented a couple of times on matrix about that but if we reexec consistently there that is concerning, it means the pause process is either killed all the time or something in reexec things we cannot use the short cut.

It would be great if we can get a VM just like the gating env but I assume this is a dream?

I agree the exe part is totally fine so I don't see how we would need any of the debug here, the more interesting part would be to confirm the parent process to see if we did indeed reexec ourselves.

---
code wise we could overwrite "comm" in our code after reexec to podman if we dislike the exe part but honestly maybe just make the test allow exe as I see this as normal.
Or maybe do not use comm at all and check in cmdline for the right arguments?

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Reproduces reliably on rawhide 1mt with `podman-5.2.2-2.fc42` installed from dnf:
```console
# loginctl enable-linger fedora
# su - fedora
$ bats --filter policies /usr/share/podman/test/system/260-sdnotify.bats
 [ fails with exe ]
```
Which of course raises the question, why does this pass in our CI?

> Reproduces reliably on rawhide 1mt with `podman-5.2.2-2.fc42` installed from dnf:
> 
> ```
> # loginctl enable-linger fedora
> # su - fedora
> $ bats --filter policies /usr/share/podman/test/system/260-sdnotify.bats
>  [ fails with exe ]
> ```
> 
> Which of course raises the question, why does this pass in our CI?

Ah we do not have XDG_RUNTIME_DIR set correctly there, do we? In this case the shortcut cannot finds its pause pidfile and thus never do the short cut and always has to do the "expensive" reexec but I have to try to confirm.

upstream CI uses a proper ssh based login session for the user so the envs are all set there.
Blocking this in preference to #24073. I think that's a better approach, although it loses us a little bit of test coverage (of podman defaults). If that one merges, I will close this.
